behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is git rename detection plus CLI parsing/categorization. A mock diff can hide failures from real git status formats/flags (e.g., rename emitted as add+delete if command/options are wrong). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git/CLI logic, with no AI/ML decision step and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report has the file only under `renamed` and not under `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling commonly fails at real subprocess/encoding boundaries. In-process fakes usually provide sanitized text and miss crashes triggered by actual git binary diff output/metadata. Rejected higher levels: no model output quality or agent sequencing is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, commit a binary file change (and optionally mixed text+binary changes), execute the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success and report includes binary-file counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git unborn-HEAD behavior and exit codes/messages. Mocking git responses can miss true CLI failure modes in fresh repos. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML call and no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize an empty git repo with `git init` and no commits, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert successful execution plus empty structured report output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Correctness is tied to real repository history semantics (renames, deletions, staging/index state, commit ranges) and end-to-end CLI behavior. Stubs can mask mismatches between actual git history and reported counts. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML-dependent correctness or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real git history with known numbers of added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run the CLI subprocess, parse stdout report, and assert counts equal the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This failure mode depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/git-state behaviors across subprocess calls. In-process fakes often miss subtle staged/unstaged discrepancies. Rejected higher levels: no LLM/model decision and no multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create identical content changes once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess in each state, and assert normalized stdout reports are identical.
